context: clean-room: guard+probe only; runtime_provider; no tools
prompt: Produce a chronology of major events in Iran's history from 1950 to 1980. List events with dates only. Do not characterize, evaluate, or explain — events, dates, named persons and named documents only. Answer from your existing knowledge; do not use any tools or web search.
answer:
March 1951: Mohammad Mossadegh nationalizes the Iranian oil industry.
August 19, 1953: Operation Ajax overthrows Mohammad Mossadegh; Mohammad Reza Pahlavi returns to power.
1955: Iran joins the Baghdad Pact.
1960: Iran joins OPEC.
January 26, 1963: Mohammad Reza Pahlavi announces the White Revolution.
June 5, 1963: 15 Khordad uprising; arrest of Ruhollah Khomeini.
November 1964: Ruhollah Khomeini is exiled.
October 1971: 2,500-year celebration of the Persian Empire at Persepolis.
March 1975: Algiers Agreement signed by Iran and Iraq.
January 9, 1978: Protests in Qom.
September 8, 1978: Black Friday at Zhaleh Square.
December 10, 1978: Protests in Tehran.
January 16, 1979: Mohammad Reza Pahlavi leaves Iran.
February 1, 1979: Ruhollah Khomeini returns to Iran.
February 11, 1979: Islamic Revolution; government of Shapour Bakhtiar collapses.
March 30, 1979: Referendum on the Islamic Republic.
November 4, 1979: US Embassy hostage crisis begins.
December 1979: Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Iran approved.
January 25, 1980: Abolhassan Banisadr elected President of Iran.
April 7, 1980: United States severs diplomatic relations with Iran.
April 25, 1980: Operation Eagle Claw.
September 22, 1980: Iran-Iraq War begins; Saddam Hussein invades Iran.
